description: The AGLedger-HELD gate for releasing a deployment that GitHub environment protection is holding in status:waiting. The principal renders the Verdict here FIRST; only after the record settles does the seam POST {state:approved} to GitHub's pending_deployments endpoint (or {state:rejected} on a reject verdict). The AGLedger record is the authorization; GitHub's approval log then reflects its execution. This composes the two gates WITHOUT duplicating the decision: when the org prefers the human to decide inside GitHub's own approval UI, skip this type and notarize the rendered outcome with deployment-approval-v1 instead, one decision, one holder. | Release agent = performer (submits the release proposal as the completion); release manager = principal (accept/reject via /verdict). | ENGINE RULES (fieldMappings, one per rule below): headsha-echo and environment-echo require the proposal to echo the authorized headSha and environment exactly, so what was approved is provably what was released to where. Both are string-typed exact-equality rules; each carries a DISTINCT free-form ruleId (dispatching to the string default verb, exact) because the engine keys rules by ruleId and two mappings sharing a verb-form ruleId silently collapse to one (F-794). In principal mode the engine result is advisory; the principal still decides. | GATE + SoD ARE ORCHESTRATOR-ENFORCED, NOT TYPE-ENFORCED (see README): gateMode:auto on create can override defaultGateMode:principal (F-791/api#838), and principal==performer self-commitment is not rejected (F-758/api#759). The orchestrator MUST NOT set gateMode:auto on this type and MUST provision distinct agent and principal identities. GitHub's own environment required-reviewers (optionally with prevent_self_review) is an independent second control line on the SoR side.

repo.description: GitHub owner/name the deployment runs in.

runId.description: GitHub Actions workflow run id currently held in status:waiting by environment protection.

environment.description: Target environment name (e.g. production). Engine requires the completion to echo it.

headSha.description: The commit the held run would deploy. Engine requires the completion to echo it.

changeRef.description: recordId of the change-authored-v1 that produced this deployable (chain lineage), when the deploy follows a tracked change.

ciRef.description: recordId of the ci-conclusion-v1 for this commit (chain lineage).

mergeAuthorizationRef.description: recordId of the merge-authorization-v1 (or review-verdict-v1 relay) whose merge produced the commit being deployed, so the deploy decision's lineage to the merge decision is a typed edge rather than free text.

rollbackOfRef.description: recordId of the delivery-outcome-v1 (deployed) being rolled back, when this authorization redeploys a previous commit.

changeRefs.description: recordIds of every change-authored-v1 this deploy releases, for train/batch deploys. Use changeRef for the single-change case.

headSha.description: The commit the performer proposes to release. Engine-checked (exact string equality) against the authorized headSha.

environment.description: The environment the performer proposes to release to. Engine-checked (exact string equality) against the gate's environment.

rationale.description: Why this release should proceed now. What the principal reads before deciding.
